title: M1: Skeleton & config plane
description: Umbrella for WS-A. Stand up package, entry point, bootstrap event wiring, config loader/validator, logger, and the single computed enabled-set. After this lands, WS-C/D/E/F/G can begin in parallel where dependencies allow. See docs/plans/pi-blackbytes-mvp.md §4 WS-A and §8 Slice 1. Note: deps says 'blocks' meaning M0 must be closed first.
close_reason: All M1 children complete: skeleton, config, handlers, enabled-set, test harness

title: M1.1: Initialize npm package skeleton (package.json, tsconfig, deps, build)
description:
## Objective
Create the baseline npm package skeleton for the extension so later leaves can build against a real package: `package.json`, `tsconfig.json`, `.gitignore`, and a stub `README.md`, with the Pi metadata fields required by PRD §4.1.

## Context
This is the first concrete WS-A implementation bead. All later source files, tests, and build outputs assume this package contract exists and that `dist/index.js` is the published extension entry.

## Scope
IN:
- `package.json` for `@blackbytes/pi-blackbytes` version `0.1.0`
- `pi.extensions: ["./dist/index.js"]`
- `pi.skills: ["./skills/**/SKILL.md"]`
- strict Node 20+ ESM TypeScript config
- baseline scripts for `build` and `test`
- dependency/peerDependency wiring:
  - dependency: `@sinclair/typebox ^0.33`
  - dependency: `zod ^4`
  - peerDependency: `@mariozechner/pi-coding-agent ^0.67`
OUT:
- extension bootstrap code, tools, handlers, or real docs content

## Implementation Context
- Files touched: `package.json`, `tsconfig.json`, `.gitignore`, `README.md`.
- Build output target must be compatible with a future `src/index.ts` compiled to `dist/index.js`.
- Keep the layout minimal; only add scripts/config needed by immediate downstream beads.

## Constraints
- Runtime target: Node >= 20.
- Package size budget (<500KB gzipped) is verified later in M6, not here.
- Reuse built-in npm/TypeScript tooling; do not introduce extra packaging layers unless required for the build to work.

## DoD
- `npm install` succeeds.
- `npm run build` emits `dist/index.js` from the package skeleton.
- Package metadata matches PRD §4.1 exactly enough for Pi to discover the extension and bundled skills.
close_reason: Implemented: package skeleton complete

title: M1.2: Implement extension entry point and bootstrap scaffold
description:
## Objective
Create the extension entry point and bootstrap scaffold: `src/index.ts` exports `default function (pi: ExtensionAPI): void`, and `src/bootstrap.ts` wires the event subscriptions required by PRD §4.3.

## Context
Pi discovers the extension through the default export. Bootstrap is the single composition point where session lifecycle hooks and future tool registration will be connected. This bead should leave obvious integration seams for later leaves without implementing their real logic yet.

## Scope
IN:
- `src/index.ts` that delegates to `bootstrap(pi)`
- `src/bootstrap.ts` with `pi.on(...)` subscriptions for:
  - `session_start`
  - `resources_discover`
  - `before_agent_start`
  - `model_select`
  - `before_provider_request`
  - `tool_call`
  - `tool_result`
  - `session_shutdown`
- each handler wrapped in a shared safe-execution pattern that logs failures and surfaces `ctx.ui.notify("error", ...)` when context is available
OUT:
- real handler behavior, enabled-set computation, and concrete tool registration

## Implementation Context
- Prefer importing placeholder handler functions from dedicated modules or obvious TODO stubs so later leaves can fill them in without reshaping bootstrap.
- Add a small startup log such as `pi-blackbytes vX.Y loaded` using package version metadata or a local constant.
- This bead should define the integration skeleton, not business logic.

## Constraints
- Handler failures must never crash the extension.
- Do not register tools here yet; that happens after the enabled-set strategy is implemented.
- Keep the scaffold simple enough that later leaves can replace placeholders incrementally.

## DoD
- Pi can load the extension without throwing.
- All required PRD §4.3 subscriptions exist in bootstrap.
- A minimal test or smoke harness proves the default export invokes bootstrap and the scaffold loads cleanly.
close_reason: Implemented: extension entry point (src/index.ts), bootstrap scaffold (src/bootstrap.ts) with all 8 PRD §4.3 event subscriptions wrapped in safe-execution pattern, placeholder handlers (src/handlers/index.ts), types (src/types/pi.ts). 2 tests pass.

title: M1.3: Implement buffered file logger with daily rotation (10MB cap)
description:
## Objective
Port the buffered file logger from oc-blackbytes into `src/shared/logger.ts`. Logs to `~/.pi/logs/pi-blackbytes.log` (fallback `/tmp/pi-blackbytes.log`), buffered writes, daily rotation, 10MB max per file.

## Context
PRD §6 logging requirement. Used by every other workstream for diagnostics. Must redact secrets (Decision 8).

## Scope
IN: logger module with levels (debug/info/warn/error), buffered append, flush on `session_shutdown`, daily rotation, size cap, secret-redaction helper for known fields (`api_key`, `authorization`, `exa_api_key`, `tavily_api_key`, `x-initiator` is fine).
OUT: integration into handlers (later).

## Implementation Context
- Expected files: `src/shared/logger.ts`, any small rotation/redaction helpers, and tests.
- The logger should be reusable from handlers, commands, and nested-runner code without forcing callers to know filesystem details.
- Shutdown flush wiring lands in a later bead, so expose an explicit flush API now.

## Constraints
- NEVER write secret values: known sensitive keys must be redacted to `***`.
- Must not block the event loop on writes.
- Failure to write log MUST NOT throw.

## DoD
- Unit tests: rotation triggers at size, secrets redacted, flush works on shutdown.
close_reason: Implemented: buffered file logger (src/shared/logger.ts) with debug/info/warn/error levels, 4KB buffer with 5s flush interval, daily rotation, 10MB size cap, secret redaction for api_key/authorization/exa_api_key/tavily_api_key. 7 tests pass.

title: M1.4: Implement Zod v4 config schema (BlackbytesConfigSchema)
description:
## Objective
Port the config Zod schema from oc-blackbytes into `src/config/schema.ts`. Schema per PRD §4.7:
- `disabled_tools: string[]` optional
- `disabled_sub_agents: enum array (explore|oracle|librarian|general)` optional
- `hashline_edit: boolean` default true
- `copilot_initiator_header: boolean` default true
- `websearch: { provider: 'exa'|'tavily', exa_api_key, tavily_api_key }` optional
- `context7: { api_key }` optional
- `sub_agents: record<string, { model, reasoningEffort, temperature }>` optional

## Context
The config block lives at `~/.pi/agent/settings.json › blackbytes`. This schema is the contract for `session_start` parsing, enabled-set computation, and every downstream feature that reads Blackbytes config.

## Scope
IN:
- `src/config/schema.ts` with Zod v4
- default values
- exports `BlackbytesConfig` type and `parseBlackbytesConfig(unknown)` returning `{ ok: true; value } | { ok: false; errors }`
- tests for validation behavior and defaults
OUT:
- file IO / settings loading (next leaf)

## Implementation Context
- Reuse the oc-blackbytes schema shape where it matches the approved PRD, but align names/defaults to this repo's canonical config block.
- The parser result should be ergonomic for `session_start`: valid config yields typed data, invalid config yields user-displayable errors without throwing.
- Unknown-key behavior matters because users may keep extra settings in their JSON; preserve them per the contract rather than silently stripping the whole object.

## Constraints
- Use Zod v4.
- Validate only the `blackbytes` subtree, not the whole Pi settings file.
- Unknown keys ignored (not stripped).
- Error output should be clear enough for UI notification / troubleshooting.

## DoD
- Unit tests: empty object validates with defaults; invalid types report clear errors; unknown keys ignored (not stripped).
- Exported type/parser signatures are stable enough for later config-loader leaves to consume directly.
close_reason: Implemented: Zod v4 config schema (src/config/schema.ts) with BlackbytesConfigSchema, BlackbytesConfig type, parseBlackbytesConfig function. All PRD §4.7 fields with defaults, passthrough for unknown keys. 5 tests pass.

title: M1.5: Implement settings loader with safe IO and atomic-friendly read
description:
## Objective
Implement the config loader that reads Pi settings, extracts the `blackbytes` block, validates it, and returns a safe parsed config without mutating unrelated settings.

## Context
This loader is called during `session_start` and feeds the enabled-set computation plus later handler/tool behavior. It must be robust to missing files, malformed JSON, and invalid user config while preserving the rule that handler failures never crash Pi.

## Scope
IN:
- `src/config/loader.ts`
- read path resolution:
  - `process.env.PI_AGENT_DIR/settings.json` when `PI_AGENT_DIR` is set
  - otherwise `~/.pi/agent/settings.json`
- extract only the top-level `blackbytes` object
- validate via `BlackbytesConfigSchema`
- return defaults when the file is missing, malformed, absent `blackbytes`, or contains invalid `blackbytes` data
- log/notify failure modes without leaking secret values
OUT:
- writing settings (`/setup-models`), JSONC support, or mutation of non-`blackbytes` settings

## Implementation Context
- Expected collaborators: schema from `M1.4`, logger from `M1.3`, bootstrap session-start flow from `M1.2`.
- Keep the loader API simple for downstream use, e.g. `loadBlackbytesConfig(): Promise<BlackbytesConfig>` (exact signature can vary).
- Document where defaults live so later beads reuse the same source.

## Constraints
- Parse strict JSON only; no JSONC/comments/trailing-comma support.
- Never log raw secret values or full provider credential objects.
- The loader must return a safe config/defaults result instead of throwing.

## DoD
- Tests cover: missing file, malformed JSON, missing `blackbytes`, invalid `blackbytes`, valid config.
- Failure paths notify/log with redacted details only.
- The returned config is ready for direct use by `session_start` without extra validation.
close_reason: Implemented: settings loader (src/config/loader.ts) reads PI_AGENT_DIR or ~/.pi/agent/settings.json, extracts blackbytes block, validates via schema, returns defaults on any failure. 5 tests pass.

title: M1.6: Compute single enabled-set for tools/sub-agents/skills at session_start
description:
## Objective
At `session_start`, compute the single immutable enabled-set for the session and expose it through a shared accessor used by registration, discovery, prompt/resource exposure, and status surfaces.

## Context
This bead implements Decision 1 after `M0.Q3` confirms the allowed activation strategy. The enabled-set is the anti-drift mechanism: every consumer must agree on the same tools, sub-agents, and skills so the model is never shown capabilities it cannot actually invoke.

## Scope
IN:
- session-start computation flow that reads validated config and derives the enabled set
- shared accessor such as `getEnabledSet(): { tools: Set<string>; subAgents: Set<string>; skills: Set<string> }`
- support for `disabled_tools` (exact public tool names) and `disabled_sub_agents` (delegate persona names)
- immutable per-session behavior; no recomputation in random modules
- implementation strategy chosen by `M0.Q3`:
  - either register/expose only enabled entries
  - or register broadly then narrow through the Pi API, if proven correct
OUT:
- actual per-tool registration logic, `<available_resources>` rendering, and `/blackbytes-status` output formatting

## Implementation Context
- Expected collaborators: config loader (`M1.5`), bootstrap/session_start scaffold (`M1.2`), future registration helper (`M2a.0`), and handler/resource surfaces in WS-E/WS-G.
- Define clearly where session-scoped state lives and how later handlers read it without recomputing.
- If the chosen strategy has Pi API caveats, encode them directly in the accessor/registration flow rather than in comments alone.

## Constraints
- Single source of truth: no duplicated enable/disable logic elsewhere.
- Must follow the exact strategy proven in `M0.Q3`.
- MVP does not support mid-session toggling.

## DoD
- Unit tests prove `disabled_tools` and `disabled_sub_agents` are applied correctly.
- Tests prove the enabled-set is computed once per session and reused.
- Integration or smoke test verifies `session_start` completes within the intended budget (<200ms with realistic config).
close_reason: Implemented enabled-set module

title: M1.7: Add baseline integration test harness (Pi mock + session_start happy/error paths)
description:
## Objective
Stand up the integration test harness used by all later workstreams: a minimal mock of `ExtensionAPI` (`registerTool`, `registerCommand`, `on`, `setActiveTools`, `appendEntry`) so handlers can be exercised in tests. Cover `session_start` with valid config and with malformed settings.

## Context
PRD §6 mandates integration tests for the critical extension surfaces, each with success and error paths. This leaf provides the reusable harness and first pair of session-start tests so later beads can add focused coverage instead of rebuilding the Pi mock each time.

## Scope
IN:
- `test/test-utils/pi-mock.ts`
- two `session_start` tests (success path, malformed-settings path)
- test script entry / wiring so the suite runs in CI
OUT:
- per-handler test matrices for other events (covered by later leaves)

## Implementation Context
- The mock should cover only the ExtensionAPI surface Blackbytes actually uses in MVP.
- Later M3/M4/M6 test beads will extend this harness, so keep helper names and event simulation APIs stable and reusable.
- Success-path config should exercise defaults plus one or two non-default settings to prove parsing/bootstrapping works.

## Constraints
- Keep the mock lightweight; no need to emulate Pi internals unrelated to this repo's handlers.
- Error-path coverage should verify graceful handling of malformed settings rather than uncaught throws.
- Avoid locking tests to incidental log strings unless those strings are part of the contract.

## DoD
- `npm test` runs and passes both `session_start` tests.
- Harness is reusable enough that later handler-test beads can add coverage without replacing the mock.
close_reason: Implemented: Pi mock harness + 2 session_start integration tests

description: Umbrella for WS-E. Implements the handlers that make Bytes feel like Bytes. Depends on bundled+http tools so the resources block lists real tool names. See plan §4 WS-E, §8 Slice 3.

title: M3.1: Hashline rewrite of Pi 'read' tool output (LINE#ID anchors)
description:
## Objective
Subscribe `tool_result` event; when `event.toolName === "read"` and `!event.isError` and `cfg.hashline_edit === true`, rewrite the text content block into `LINE#ID|content` anchored format and return updated content.

## Context
PRD §4.5, plan Decision 5. This is what makes `hashline_edit` precise — the LLM sees stable line anchors. Hashline rewrite scope is NARROW: read tool output only; do NOT touch Pi's `edit` tool output.

## Scope
IN: `src/handlers/tool-result.ts` (read branch), helper to compute deterministic 2-letter CID from set `ZPMQVRWSNKTXJBYH` per line, reuse oc-blackbytes anchor format `{n}#{ID}|{content}`.
OUT: write rewrite (next leaf), edit tool changes (forbidden).

## Implementation Context
- Expected touch points: `src/handlers/tool-result.ts`, a shared hashline-anchor helper, and tests under the M1.7 harness.
- Reuse the same anchor format consumed by the `hashline_edit` tool so search/edit workflows compose without translation.
- Performance matters because every successful `read` may pass through this path; keep the transformation linear and lightweight.

## Constraints
- Only rewrite text payloads; skip non-text/binary content blocks.
- Preserve `isError` responses verbatim.
- Skip if `cfg.hashline_edit` is false.
- Guard against malformed input shape — never throw.
- < 50ms average overhead per `tool_result` (PRD §6).
- Hashline scope is ONLY `read` and `write`; do NOT touch Pi's `edit` output.

## DoD
- Unit tests: happy path, `isError` preserved, non-text skipped, malformed input safe, perf microbenchmark assertion.
close_reason: Implemented: tool-result handler with hashline read rewrite + write normalize

title: M3.2: Hashline normalize of Pi 'write' tool output (line-count summary)
description:
## Objective
In `src/handlers/tool-result.ts`, when `event.toolName === "write"` and `!event.isError` and `cfg.hashline_edit === true`, replace the result content with a single text block: `"File written successfully. {N} lines written."`.

## Context
PRD §4.5 normalizes write output so the LLM does not get echoed file content after a successful write. This keeps context cleaner and aligns write behavior with the broader hashline workflow without modifying Pi's native edit tool semantics.

## Scope
IN:
- write branch of the tool-result handler
- line-count derivation from the write event payload
- tests for normalization and skip paths
OUT:
- read-result hashline rewrite (separate leaf)
- any changes to generic tool-result behavior for unrelated tools

## Implementation Context
- Expected touch points: `src/handlers/tool-result.ts`, any shared hashline normalization helper, and tests in the harness from M1.7.
- Derive the line count from the actual write payload/content, not from filesystem rereads.
- Keep the rewritten output shape stable because prompts may rely on this exact summary pattern.

## Constraints
- Skip if `hashline_edit` false.
- Preserve `isError`.
- Hashline scope ONLY read/write; never touch edit.
- Successful writes should summarize content, not echo it.

## DoD
- Unit tests: happy path, `isError` preserved, `hashline_edit=false` skipped.
- Test covers accurate line-count calculation for multi-line writes.
close_reason: Implemented: tool-result handler with hashline read rewrite + write normalize

title: M3.3: Inject <available_resources> + language-matching block in before_agent_start (idempotent)
description:
## Objective
Subscribe `before_agent_start`; build the `<available_resources>` block listing exactly the enabled-set (tools + skills + sub-agent personas: bytes, explore, oracle, librarian, general) and append it (plus the language-matching block) to `event.systemPrompt`. MUST be idempotent across turns.

## Context
PRD §4.6, §6 (idempotency), plan Decision 1 (single enabled-set) + Decision 6 (idempotent augmentation).

## Scope
IN: `src/handlers/before-agent-start.ts`. Wrap the block in a stable sentinel comment pair (e.g., `<!-- pi-blackbytes:resources:start -->` ... `<!-- pi-blackbytes:resources:end -->`). Detect the sentinel before appending; if present, replace the inner block instead of appending again.
OUT: model-select cache (separate leaf).

## Implementation Context
- Expected touch points: `src/handlers/before-agent-start.ts`, enabled-set reader from M1.6, any prompt-block builder helper, and tests.
- The rendered list must come from the single enabled-set so prompt-visible resources stay aligned with actual runtime availability.
- Keep replacement logic deterministic so repeated turns update the inner block without duplicating sentinel wrappers.

## Constraints
- Tool/persona names listed MUST match PRD §5.1 verbatim: `hashline_edit`, `ast_grep_search`, `ast_grep_replace`, `grep`, `glob`, `websearch_search`, `websearch_fetch`, `context7_resolve_library_id`, `context7_query_docs`, `grep_app_search_github`, `delegate_explore`, `delegate_oracle`, `delegate_librarian`, `delegate_general`.
- Reads from `getEnabledSet()` — never re-derive.
- Idempotent: repeated calls in the same session do not append duplicates.

## DoD
- Unit tests: first-turn appends, second-turn replaces in-place, disabled tool excluded, sentinel format stable.
close_reason: Available resources injection with sentinel-based idempotency

title: M3.4: Inject x-initiator: agent header for GitHub Copilot in before_provider_request
description:
## Objective
Subscribe `before_provider_request`; if `ctx.model.provider` is GitHub Copilot AND `cfg.copilot_initiator_header` is true, mutate the outgoing payload headers to add `x-initiator: agent`.

## Context
GitHub Copilot provider requests need the `x-initiator: agent` header in this workflow. The exact mutation mechanism is locked by M0.Q2, so this implementation bead should copy the confirmed payload shape instead of re-researching header semantics.

## Scope
IN:
- `src/handlers/before-provider-request.ts` header branch
- skip path when config disabled or provider not Copilot
- tests for add / skip / preserve-existing behavior
OUT:
- reasoning-effort mapping (next leaf)

## Implementation Context
- Reuse the provider-request event shape and mutation pattern recorded in `docs/decisions/m0-feasibility.md`.
- Expected touch points: provider-request handler, shared model/provider helpers if needed, bootstrap wiring, and tests.
- Keep this header logic separate from the reasoning params branch so failures are easier to reason about.

## Constraints
- Only mutate when provider is Copilot.
- Never overwrite existing `x-initiator` if user-set.
- Failure must be caught + logged; not crash provider call.

## DoD
- Unit test: header added for copilot, not added otherwise, not overwritten if present.
- Implementation follows the exact mutation pattern confirmed by M0.Q2 rather than introducing a new transport wrapper.
close_reason: Copilot x-initiator header via registerProvider at session_start

title: M3.5: Cache active model family on model_select
description:
## Objective
Subscribe `model_select`; classify the active model into a family (`claude|gpt|gemini|other`) and cache it for use by the reasoning-effort mapper.

## Context
M3.6 needs a stable provider-family lookup so it can translate Blackbytes `reasoningEffort` settings into provider-specific request params. This bead owns the model-family state and invalidation rules, separate from the mutation logic itself.

## Scope
IN:
- `src/handlers/model-select.ts` with module-level cache
- family inference helper in `src/shared/model-capability.ts`
- tests for family classification and cache update behavior
OUT:
- actual provider-request param mutation (next leaf)

## Implementation Context
- The cached value should be readable by the `before_provider_request` handler without recomputing from scratch each time.
- Known families for MVP are Anthropic/Claude, OpenAI/GPT, Google/Gemini, plus a safe `other` bucket.
- Keep the helper reusable for other provider-capability checks if needed.

## Constraints
- Cache must be refreshed on every `model_select` event.
- Unknown model IDs should map to `other`, not throw.
- Avoid provider-specific network calls or heavy parsing; classification should be local and deterministic.

## DoD
- Unit test: family inference for known model IDs (`claude-*`, `gpt-*`, `gemini-*`).
- Test also covers an unknown model mapping to `other` and cache replacement on repeated `model_select` events.
close_reason: Implemented: model family classification + cache on model_select

title: M3.6: Map reasoningEffort to provider-family thinking/reasoning params in before_provider_request
description:
## Objective
In `src/handlers/before-provider-request.ts` (params branch), translate `reasoningEffort` (`low|medium|high`) into the correct provider-family params: Anthropic `thinking`, OpenAI `reasoning_effort`, Google `thinking_config`. Only intervene where Pi does not already handle it (PRD G6, ~90% parity).

## Context
Blackbytes exposes one cross-provider `reasoningEffort` setting, but actual request params vary by provider family. This leaf bridges that gap using the model-family cache from M3.5 while staying idempotent when Pi already injected equivalent settings.

## Scope
IN:
- param-mapping helper + handler glue
- detection of already-present provider-native params
- tests for per-family mapping and no-op paths
OUT:
- header mutation branch (separate leaf)

## Implementation Context
- Expected touch points: `src/handlers/before-provider-request.ts`, `src/shared/model-capability.ts`, and tests.
- Use the cached family from M3.5 rather than recomputing from raw model IDs in multiple places.
- Output/request mutations should stay narrowly scoped to the provider-native reasoning fields.

## Constraints
- Idempotent: don't double-set.
- Per-family logic in `src/shared/model-capability.ts`.
- Unsupported family should no-op safely.
- Do not clobber unrelated request params.

## DoD
- Unit tests: each family maps correctly; no-op when already set; unsupported family is skipped.
- Tests cover low/medium/high translation shape at the contract level rather than only one hard-coded value.
close_reason: Implemented

title: M3.7: Wire session_shutdown to flush logger
description:
## Objective
Subscribe `session_shutdown` and force any buffered logger state to flush before the extension exits.

## Context
This closes the logging lifecycle started in M1.3. Blackbytes keeps a buffered file logger for observability, so shutdown must not drop trailing entries when Pi tears down the session. PRD §5.2 expects logger writes to be durable across normal shutdown.

## Scope
IN:
- wire a `session_shutdown` handler
- invoke the shared logger flush path exactly once per shutdown event
- cover the shutdown path with a unit/integration-style test using the Pi mock harness
OUT:
- changing rotation, file paths, or normal append behavior from M1.3

## Implementation Context
- Expected touch points: `src/handlers/session-shutdown.ts` (or equivalent), logger module created in M1.3, bootstrap registration, and tests under the harness from M1.7.
- Reuse the existing logger abstraction; do not create a second shutdown-specific writer.
- If the logger exposes async flush semantics, the handler should await them before returning.

## Constraints
- Shutdown handling must be safe when logging is disabled or the logger has not been initialized.
- Multiple shutdown signals must not duplicate writes or throw.
- Failures should be swallowed/logged safely so teardown itself does not crash.

## DoD
- Unit test: shutdown flushes pending writes.
- Test also covers the no-op path when no logger is active or nothing is buffered.
- Registration path is explicit enough that later leaves can rely on flush-on-shutdown behavior without rereading the plan.
close_reason: Session shutdown flushes logger buffer

title: M4.0: Port persona prompts (bytes default/gpt/gemini, explore, oracle, librarian, general)
description:
## Objective
Port persona prompt markdowns from oc-blackbytes into `src/prompts/`:
- `bytes/{default,gpt,gemini}.md` (global system-prompt augmentation; not exposed as `delegate_*`)
- `explore.md`, `oracle.md`, `librarian.md`, `general.md` (used by `delegate_*` runner)

## Context
Blackbytes' delegation model depends on prompt assets that match each persona's role and runtime allowlist. The bytes family prompts shape the primary session behavior, while the sub-agent prompts are loaded by the nested runner for explore/oracle/librarian/general.

## Scope
IN:
- 7 markdown files under `src/prompts/`
- sub-agent prompts include the language-matching block and honest allowlist descriptions
- bytes prompt variants for default/gpt/gemini provider families
OUT:
- runtime loading/wiring of the prompts (separate leaves)

## Implementation Context
- Source material is oc-blackbytes; port the working content, then adjust names/tool references for this repo's canonical surface.
- Prompt text must stay consistent with M0.Q4 allowlists and M0.Q5 nested-runner contract.
- Keep file names and directory layout stable so later loader code can reference them deterministically.

## Constraints
- Each sub-agent prompt MUST describe its allowlist truthfully, even though enforcement happens at runtime.
- Do not expose the bytes prompts as delegate tools.
- Preserve language-matching behavior and project-operating style from the source prompts.

## DoD
- Files committed; load test reads them at runtime without error.
- Prompt set is complete enough that later delegate-tool leaves can wire it in without re-authoring persona guidance.
close_reason: Implemented: 7 persona prompt markdowns (bytes default/gpt/gemini + explore/oracle/librarian/general)

title: M4.1: Implement nested Pi runner (runNestedPi) per M0 strategy with cancellation, timeout, allowlist enforcement, recursion guard
description:
## Objective
Implement `src/sub-agents/runner.ts` exporting `runNestedPi({ systemPrompt, userPrompt, model, reasoningEffort, allowedTools, cwd, signal, onUpdate })` per the M0.Q1 chosen execution path (programmatic `AgentSession` or `pi -p` subprocess), with the M0.Q5 result/cancellation/timeout contract and the M0.Q4 runtime allowlist enforcement.

## Context
WS-F core. Plan Decisions 2 (runtime allowlist), 3 (`maxDepth=1`). Runner is consumed by all 4 `delegate_*` tools. Strategy and contract MUST follow `docs/decisions/m0-feasibility.md` verbatim — do NOT improvise.

## Scope
IN: runner module, recursion guard (e.g., env var or context flag the runner sets so the spawned session does NOT register `delegate_*` tools), default timeout, parent-signal cancellation propagation, env inheritance allowlist, structured failure normalization (success: `{ content, details }`; failure: structured text + machine-readable details).
OUT: per-persona `delegate_*` tools (next leaves).

## Implementation Context
- Expected files: `src/sub-agents/runner.ts`, shared nested-result types/helpers, delegate-tool call sites, and tests.
- The runner should be the single place where timeout, cancellation, env inheritance, recursion depth, and allowlist rules are enforced so delegate leaves stay thin.
- Match the exact session strategy, error codes, and streaming/buffering policy recorded in the M0 decision doc.

## Constraints
- Allowlist enforced at RUNTIME, not just prompt (Decision 2). If subprocess path can't enforce, MUST fall back to programmatic per M0.Q1.
- `maxDepth = 1`: spawned sessions must not be able to call `delegate_*` again.
- Cancellation propagates from parent `AbortSignal`.
- `cwd` inherits from parent context; env inherits ONLY allowlisted vars per M0.Q5 record.
- Streaming optional; if unsupported, buffer and return single final result with same schema.

## Risks
- If chosen path leaks tools into the nested session beyond allowlist, fail loudly in tests.

## DoD
- Integration tests: success result, timeout, cancellation, allowlist-violation rejected, recursion-guard prevents nested `delegate_*` call.
close_reason: Nested Pi runner with recursion guard, timeout, allowlist, cancellation

title: M2a.0: Shared TypeBox schema helpers + tool registration helper
description:
## Objective
Create the shared registration and schema helpers that every bundled/local tool implementation will build on: a guarded `registerTool` wrapper plus common TypeBox fragments for repeated parameter shapes.

## Context
This bead is the control point that prevents individual tool leaves from bypassing the enabled-set. It also keeps schema boilerplate consistent across the bundled tools port.

## Scope
IN:
- `src/tools/_shared/register-tool.ts` (or equivalent canonical path) that wraps `pi.registerTool`
- enabled-set gate based on the public tool name
- shared TypeBox helpers for repeated shapes such as paths, globs, grep-style search params, and filesystem target lists
- lightweight helper typing so later tool beads can register without duplicating guard logic
OUT:
- concrete tool implementations and HTTP client work

## Implementation Context
- Expected collaborators: enabled-set state from `M1.6`, future bundled tools under `src/tools/**`.
- The registration helper should define the narrow contract later beads use, e.g. name/schema/handler input and skip behavior when disabled.
- Shared schemas should be only the pieces reused across multiple tools; keep tool-specific schemas in their own beads.

## Constraints
- The helper must preserve the exact canonical tool names from the PRD.
- Disabled tools should be omitted cleanly rather than registered then hidden.
- Avoid over-abstracting; this is a small shared layer, not a framework.

## DoD
- Unit test proves disabled tools are skipped and enabled tools are registered.
- At least one shared TypeBox helper is used by more than one downstream tool family by design.
- The helper API is concrete enough that WS-C/WS-D leaves can implement against it without redefining the enabled-set check.
close_reason: Implemented: registerTool wrapper with enabled-set gate + shared TypeBox schema helpers

title: M2a.1: Port hashline_edit tool (LINE#ID-anchored multi-edit)

## Context
Hashline edit is the central Bytes workflow. Reuse logic from existing oc-blackbytes `src/extensions/tools/hashline-edit/`. Schema via `@sinclair/typebox`; respect `disabled_tools`.

## Scope
IN: full tool implementation, edit operations (replace single/range, append/prepend with anchor or BOF/EOF), snapshot apply order, autocorrect for echoed prefixes/CRLF/BOM.
OUT: rewriting Pi's read tool output (that's the M3 hashline rewrite leaf).

## Implementation Context
- Expected files: `src/tools/hashline-edit/index.ts`, any hashline parser/apply helpers, shared registration/schema code from M2a.0, and tests.
- Match the existing hashline contract used by prompts and skills: LINE#ID anchors, bottom-up application, and precise range semantics.
- The tool should work with M3's read-output rewrite but remain independent from Pi's native `edit` implementation.

## Constraints
- Tool name MUST be exactly `hashline_edit` (PRD §5.1 canonical contract).
- Must NOT replace Pi's built-in `edit` tool (PRD §2.2 out-of-scope, plan Decision 5).

title: M2a.5: Port glob tool
description:
## Objective
Implement `src/tools/glob/index.ts` registering tool `glob` (canonical). Uses `fast-glob`, returns matching paths sorted by mtime, capped at 100 with 60s timeout.

## Context
This ports one of the core local-search tools from oc-blackbytes. It gives agents a safe file-discovery primitive that is faster and narrower than shelling out to `find`, and it is part of the default read/search workflow expected by prompts and skills.

## Scope
IN:

## Implementation Context

- Returned paths should be suitable for later `read` calls.

## Constraints
- Tool name exact: `glob`.
- Preserve the 100-match safety cap and timeout even when the underlying library could return more.
- Sort by mtime so the most recently touched files appear first.

## DoD

- Tool output and failure mode are specific enough for downstream prompts to rely on without shell fallback.

title: M5.1: Implement /setup-models wizard (atomic safe write to settings.json)
description:
## Objective
Implement `src/commands/setup-models.ts` registering `pi.registerCommand("setup-models", ...)`. TUI wizard using `ctx.ui.select/input/confirm` to:
- detect / pick provider(s),
- collect API keys (Exa or Tavily for websearch, Context7 optional, Copilot/OpenAI/Anthropic provider keys as relevant),
- pick default model and recommended reasoning settings,
- write `~/.pi/agent/settings.json` (or `PI_AGENT_DIR/settings.json`) preserving all unrelated keys.

## Context
Plan Decision 4 + PRD §6 Config safety. JSON only, no JSONC. Atomic write (write to temp file then rename) so concurrent Pi reads never see partial files. Must dedupe `packages` array entries. Confirm before overwriting any existing key in the `blackbytes` block.

## Scope
IN: wizard flow, merge algorithm for `blackbytes` block, atomic write helper, dedupe packages, JSON validation pre-write, no-secret logging.
OUT: `/blackbytes-status` (separate optional leaf).

## Implementation Context
- Expected files: `src/commands/setup-models.ts`, config read/write helpers, logger/redaction reuse, and tests.
- The command should read the latest settings immediately before merge so interactive changes do not clobber unrelated edits.
- Keep provider/model questions aligned with the actual config schema from 0d5.4 and the enabled external-tool surface.

## Constraints
- JSON only — must NOT emit JSONC.
- Preserve all keys outside `blackbytes` byte-for-byte.
- Atomic write: temp file + `fs.rename`.
- Re-read settings immediately before merge to minimize lost-update window.
- Never log secret values entered by user (use redaction from logger).
- Confirm prompt before overwriting any existing `blackbytes.*` key.
- Validate `JSON.stringify` result parses back successfully before writing.

## Risks
- Settings clobber on concurrent edit — mitigated by re-read + atomic rename.

## DoD
- Integration tests: missing file (creates), malformed file (notify + abort, do not destroy), concurrent edit (re-reads), key preservation, secret-not-logged assertion, dedupe packages, confirm-before-overwrite branch.
close_reason: Implemented: setup-models wizard with atomic write, confirm-before-overwrite, dedupe packages

title: M6.3: Integration test matrix — tool_result hashline rewrite (success + error paths)

title: M6.6: CI gates — perf and size budgets
description:
## Objective
Add CI checks enforcing performance and size budgets per PRD §6 NFRs.

## Context
PRD §6 NFRs: `session_start` microbenchmark < 200ms, average `tool_result` handler overhead < 50ms, gzipped published package < 500KB.

## Scope
IN:
- `scripts/measure-startup.ts` — runs N iterations of the bootstrap (from 0d5.2) under the M1.7 mock, reports p50/p95.
- `scripts/measure-tool-result.ts` — runs N iterations of the `tool_result` handler chain (22a.1+22a.2) over a representative payload, reports average overhead.
- `scripts/check-size.sh` — `npm pack` then `gzip -c <tarball> | wc -c` and assert < 500KB.
- CI step (in `.github/workflows/ci.yml` or equivalent) that fails the build if any budget exceeded.
OUT: dashboards, historical tracking.

## Implementation Context
- Expected files: measurement scripts under `scripts/`, workflow updates in `.github/workflows/ci.yml`, and any package scripts needed to invoke them.
- Startup benchmark should exercise the actual bootstrap wiring under the Pi mock from M1.7, not an isolated toy path.
- Size check should measure the publishable tarball, not the raw repo checkout.

## Constraints
- Budgets are HARD gates; do NOT introduce a "warn-only" tier.
- Microbenchmarks must run in <30s total to keep CI fast.

## DoD
- CI fails when any budget exceeded; passes on the current MVP build.
close_reason: Implemented: CI gates for startup/tool-result perf + package size budgets

title: M0: Feasibility spike — lock nested-session, header mutation, active-tools, allowlist, transport contracts
description: Umbrella for M0 feasibility spike. Resolves PRD §9 Q1–Q3 plus allowlist enforcement and transport/cancellation contract. Output: docs/decisions/m0-feasibility.md with chosen path + fallback per question. M1 (skeleton-and-config) and M4 (sub-agents) MUST NOT begin until this closes. See docs/plans/pi-blackbytes-mvp.md §4 WS-B and §8 Slice 0.

title: M0.Q1: Choose nested Pi session execution path (programmatic AgentSession vs subprocess)
description:
## Objective
Decide the nested-session execution path for `delegate_*` tools: programmatic `AgentSession` from `@mariozechner/pi-coding-agent` (target `^0.67`) versus a `pi -p` subprocess fallback. Record the final decision in `docs/decisions/m0-feasibility.md` under `Q1: nested-session execution`.

## Context
This bead is the implementation contract for WS-F's future nested runner (`M4.1`). The chosen path must support runtime tool allowlist enforcement, parent-driven cancellation, and a hard recursion limit (`maxDepth=1`). If one path cannot satisfy those constraints cleanly, reject it and document the fallback only as contingency.

## Scope
IN:
- inspect exported APIs/types from `@mariozechner/pi-coding-agent`
- probe whether programmatic sessions can enforce tool subsets and propagate `AbortSignal`
- compare against subprocess behavior for startup cost, streaming support, cancellation, and allowlist control
- write the decision record and fallback path in `docs/decisions/m0-feasibility.md`
OUT:
- implementing the runner, delegate tools, or prompt text

## Implementation Context
- Expected research surface: package exports/types, any existing nested-session helpers, and a tiny throwaway probe if source inspection is inconclusive.
- Downstream consumers: `src/subagents/run-nested-pi.ts` (future), delegate tool handlers, and the allowlist design locked in `M0.Q4`.
- Capture the exact API or subprocess invocation shape that M4 should implement.

## Constraints
- Target Pi version: `@mariozechner/pi-coding-agent ^0.67`.
- Runtime allowlist enforcement is mandatory; prompt-only enforcement is forbidden.
- Cancellation must propagate from parent `AbortSignal`.
- Nested sessions must not be able to invoke another `delegate_*` tool (`maxDepth=1`).

## DoD
- `docs/decisions/m0-feasibility.md` contains a `Q1: nested-session execution` section with:
  - chosen path
  - rejected alternative and why
  - fallback path
  - allowlist enforcement plan
  - cancellation contract
  - streaming policy (`stream` vs buffered)
  - any startup/perf notes relevant to M4
- The note includes enough concrete API detail that M4 can proceed without rereading the original plan.
close_reason: Research: programmatic createAgentSession chosen over subprocess. SDK provides tool allowlist via tools param, cancellation via session.abort(), in-process with no startup overhead. Documented in docs/decisions/m0-feasibility.md Q1.

title: M0.Q2: Confirm before_provider_request can mutate HTTP headers (Copilot x-initiator)
description:
## Objective
Confirm whether Pi's `before_provider_request` hook can mutate outgoing HTTP headers, not just body/params, and record the exact mutation pattern in `docs/decisions/m0-feasibility.md` under `Q2: header mutation`.

## Context
WS-E uses this for the GitHub Copilot `x-initiator: agent` header (`M3.4`) and for future provider-request mutations. The goal is to lock the actual event payload shape before implementation, including whether header maps are mutable in place or require replacement.

## Scope
IN:
- inspect Pi types/source for `before_provider_request`
- run a minimal proof-of-capability probe if the type surface is ambiguous
- document the exact payload shape and mutation pattern
- define the fallback path if header mutation is unsupported
OUT:
- production handler implementation in `src/handlers/before-provider-request.ts`

## Implementation Context
- Decision doc target: `docs/decisions/m0-feasibility.md`
- Future consumer: `M3.4` must be able to copy a working snippet directly from this bead output.
- Capture whether headers are plain objects, `Headers`, or provider-specific structures, and whether mutation happens preflight or via replacement.

## Constraints
- Focus on request-header mutation only; do not broaden into unrelated provider-request logic.
- If header mutation is unsupported, document the minimum viable fallback (for example a custom transport wrapper) and what complexity it adds.

## DoD
- Decision section states `confirmed: yes|no`.
- Includes exact payload shape, one working mutation snippet, and any caveats (e.g. immutable objects, provider-specific fields, timing).
- Explicitly states the implementation rule M3.4 should follow and the fallback if unsupported.
close_reason: Research confirmed: before_provider_request cannot mutate HTTP headers (payload-only). Fallback: pi.registerProvider with headers option. Decision documented in docs/decisions/m0-feasibility.md Q2.

title: M0.Q3: Confirm pi.setActiveTools dynamism semantics
description:
## Objective
Determine whether `pi.setActiveTools` (or an equivalent Pi API) can safely change the active tool set after `session_start`, or whether the extension must compute the enabled-set once and only register/expose that subset for the entire session. Record the answer in `docs/decisions/m0-feasibility.md` under `Q3: active-tools dynamism`.

## Context
This locks Decision 1 for WS-A. `before_agent_start`, `resources_discover`, `<available_resources>`, tool registration, and `/blackbytes-status` must all read from one shared enabled-set. If post-start mutation is weak or cosmetic, the session_start-only strategy wins.

## Scope
IN:
- inspect the `ExtensionAPI` surface related to tool activation
- test both strategies if the API appears ambiguous:
  1. register all then dynamically narrow active tools
  2. compute enabled-set at `session_start` and register/expose only that subset
- document implications for tool registration and resource exposure
OUT:
- implementing the enabled-set module itself (`M1.6`)

## Implementation Context
- Downstream consumer: `src/session/enabled-set.ts` (future) plus bootstrap wiring.
- Capture whether dynamism affects only model-visible resources, only invocation permission, or both.
- The decision must be specific enough that `M1.6` can implement the correct strategy without re-research.

## Constraints
- Single source of truth is mandatory; no split between visible resources and invocable tools.
- MVP does not need mid-session enable/disable support unless Pi provides it cleanly and consistently.

## DoD
- Decision section states `dynamic: yes|no`.
- Includes chosen strategy, why the alternative was rejected, and the concrete implication for `resources_discover` and registration timing.
- Notes any API caveats or tests used to confirm behavior.
close_reason: Confirmed dynamic: yes. pi.setActiveTools() supports post-session_start tool toggling. Strategy: register all at startup, narrow with setActiveTools(). Decision documented in docs/decisions/m0-feasibility.md Q3.

title: M0.Q4: Define runtime tool-allowlist enforcement strategy for nested sessions
description:
## Objective
Define the runtime enforcement mechanism for persona-specific tool allowlists in nested sessions and document it in `docs/decisions/m0-feasibility.md` under `Q4: nested-session allowlist enforcement`.

## Context
Prompt-only enforcement is explicitly forbidden. `delegate_explore`, `delegate_oracle`, `delegate_librarian`, and `delegate_general` each need a runtime-enforced capability boundary before `M4.1` can be implemented safely.

## Scope
IN:
- pick the enforcement point that matches the Q1 session strategy (tool registration subset, invocation interception, child config, or equivalent)
- document the exact allowlist per persona
- define what happens on violation and what gets returned to the parent session
OUT:
- implementing the nested runner or delegate tools

## Implementation Context
- Future implementation areas: nested runner setup, tool registration helper, delegate tool wrappers.
- The decision must state whether enforcement happens by omission (tool never registered), denial at call time, or both.
- Include the structured error shape or response contract used when a child tries a forbidden tool.

## Constraints
- Preserve the MVP persona matrix exactly:
  - `delegate_explore`: read/search only; no write; no shell
  - `delegate_oracle`: read-only consultation; no write; no shell; web/docs only if explicitly enabled
  - `delegate_librarian`: read/search/docs/web only; no write; no shell
  - `delegate_general`: full implementation; can write/shell per parent policy
- Prompt text may reinforce policy but cannot be the only defense.

## DoD
- Decision section includes:
  - chosen enforcement mechanism
  - persona allowlist matrix verbatim
  - violation behavior and structured error contract
  - confirmation that prompt-only enforcement is not relied on
  - any limitations that M4 must preserve
close_reason: Research: enforcement by tool registration omission + beforeToolCall interception. Persona allowlist matrix defined. Prompt-only enforcement rejected. Documented in docs/decisions/m0-feasibility.md Q4.

title: M0.Q5: Define result transport, cancellation, timeout, and recursion-guard contract for delegate_* tools
description:
## Objective
Lock the execution contract for every `delegate_*` tool: result transport, cancellation propagation, timeout policy, failure envelope, environment inheritance, and recursion guard. Record it in `docs/decisions/m0-feasibility.md` under `Q5: delegate contract`.

## Context
This is the handshake between the parent session and the future nested runner in `M4.1`. Without a fixed contract, different delegate tools could drift in timeout behavior, returned shape, or recursion safety.

## Scope
IN:
- define the success envelope and failure envelope returned by delegate tools
- define how parent `AbortSignal` cancellation propagates to the child
- set a default timeout and timeout override policy
- document which env vars/config are inherited by the child
- define how `maxDepth=1` is enforced at runtime
OUT:
- implementation of the runner, prompt porting, or individual delegate tool registration

## Implementation Context
- Future implementation targets likely include `src/subagents/run-nested-pi.ts`, delegate tool modules, and shared result helpers.
- The contract should specify whether nested output is buffered before return or streamed internally then summarized.
- Capture enough TypeScript shape detail that M4 can translate it directly into interfaces/types.

## Constraints
- Default timeout should be explicit (proposal around 5 minutes unless research proves another default is safer).
- Failures must be structured and machine-readable, not free-form only.
- Recursion guard must be runtime-enforced, not prompt-enforced.

## DoD
- Decision section includes:
  - success envelope shape (`content`, `details`, and any metadata)
  - failure envelope shape and error codes/categories
  - cancellation propagation rule
  - default timeout and override story
  - env inheritance allowlist
  - recursion-guard mechanism
  - streaming vs buffered return policy
close_reason: Research: structured success/failure envelopes, 5min default timeout, PI_NESTED_DEPTH recursion guard, buffered return, cancellation via childSession.abort(). Documented in docs/decisions/m0-feasibility.md Q5.

title: M2b: HTTP-backed tools (websearch, context7, grep_app)
description: Umbrella for WS-D. Replace 3 MCP servers with 5 custom HTTP tools using canonical names from PRD §5.1. Each tool needs default timeout, normalized error envelope, secret redaction, explicit retry-or-no-retry policy. See plan §4 WS-D, §8 Slice 2.
close_reason: Epic umbrella — children unblocked

title: M2b.0: Shared HTTP client (timeout, error normalization, secret redaction, no-retry default)
description:
## Objective
Implement the shared HTTP client wrapper used by all remote-call tools so timeout handling, normalized errors, and secret redaction behave consistently across websearch, context7, and grep_app integrations.

## Context
This bead centralizes PRD §6 HTTP resilience plus the no-secret-leak requirement. Without it, each HTTP-backed tool would reinvent timeout/error/redaction behavior and drift in unsafe ways.

## Scope
IN:
- `src/tools/_shared/http.ts`
- a thin wrapper around `fetch` (or the project-standard HTTP primitive) with:
  - default timeout
  - `AbortSignal` support
  - normalized success/error result shape
  - explicit no-retry default
  - header/query/body redaction utilities for logs and surfaced errors
- opt-in redaction keys/config for tool-specific secrets
OUT:
- per-tool request/response mapping for individual HTTP tools

## Implementation Context
- Downstream users: all WS-D tool leaves (`websearch_*`, `context7_*`, `grep_app_*`).
- Collaborators: logger from `M1.3`, registration helper patterns from `M2a.0` where useful.
- Prefer a small explicit API such as `httpFetch({ url, method, headers, body, timeoutMs, signal, redactKeys })` returning a discriminated result.

## Constraints
- Default timeout should be explicit (30s is the current working assumption unless a better project default emerges).
- No automatic retries in the shared layer.
- Errors and logs must never contain raw auth headers, raw API keys, or full sensitive query strings.

## DoD
- Unit tests cover timeout, network failure, non-2xx response, and redaction of sensitive headers/query params.
- The helper returns a normalized error shape suitable for direct use by downstream tools.
- Abort/cancellation behavior is documented in the helper contract so later tools can compose it safely.
close_reason: Shared HTTP client with timeout, redaction, normalized errors

title: M2b.1: Implement websearch_search tool (Exa or Tavily based on config)
description:

Implement `src/tools/websearch/search.ts` registering tool `websearch_search` (canonical name from PRD §5.1). Calls Exa `/search` or Tavily `/search` based on `blackbytes.websearch.provider` config, using the matching API key.

OUT: fetch (separate tool).

## Implementation Context

- Result normalization should hide provider-specific payload quirks behind one stable tool contract.

title: M2b.2: Implement websearch_fetch tool (URL → markdown/text/html)
description:
## Objective
Implement `src/tools/websearch/fetch.ts` registering tool `websearch_fetch` (canonical). Fetches a URL and converts to requested format (`markdown` default).

## Context
This is the follow-up fetch primitive for search-driven workflows. After `websearch_search` surfaces candidate URLs, agents need a deterministic way to retrieve and normalize page content for analysis.

## Scope
IN:
- params `url`, `format` (`markdown|text|html`), `timeout`

- shared HTTP client usage and content-format conversion
OUT:
- search result discovery (`websearch_search`)

## Implementation Context
- Expected files: `src/tools/websearch/fetch.ts`, shared registration/schema helpers, HTTP client from M2b.0, and tests.
- Keep output concise enough for LLM consumption while preserving the requested format contract.
- Align timeout and error messaging with the rest of the web tool family.

## Constraints
- Tool name exact: `websearch_fetch`.
- Default format is `markdown`.
- Upgrade `http://` URLs to `https://` before fetch.
- Use the shared HTTP client/error normalization path.

## DoD

- Invalid URL / upstream failure paths return structured, non-crashing errors.

title: M2b.3: Implement context7_resolve_library_id tool
description:
## Objective
Implement `src/tools/context7/resolve.ts` registering tool `context7_resolve_library_id` (canonical). Resolves a library name + query to a Context7-compatible `/org/project` ID.

## Context
This is the discovery half of the Context7 integration. It lets Blackbytes map human-friendly package/library names to the exact `libraryId` required by the docs-query tool.

## Scope
IN:
- params `libraryName`, `query`
- Context7 API call through the shared HTTP client
- normalization of the best-match result and error handling
OUT:
- documentation retrieval itself (handled by `context7_query_docs`)

## Implementation Context
- Expected files: `src/tools/context7/resolve.ts`, shared schema/registration helper from M2a.0, HTTP client from M2b.0, and tests.
- Some endpoints may be public, but config may provide `blackbytes.context7.api_key`; support that path cleanly.
- Output should make the selected library ID easy for downstream tools/prompts to consume directly.

## Constraints
- Tool name exact: `context7_resolve_library_id`.
- Params `libraryName` and `query` are both in scope; do not collapse this into a docs-fetch operation.
- Normalize 4xx/5xx errors through the shared HTTP client/error contract.

## DoD

- Tool registers under the canonical name and returns a stable, machine-usable selected-library result.

title: M2b.4: Implement context7_query_docs tool
description:
## Objective
Implement `src/tools/context7/query.ts` registering tool `context7_query_docs` (canonical). Retrieves documentation/code examples from Context7 for a given `libraryId` + `query`.

## Context
This is the second half of the Context7 integration. After `context7_resolve_library_id` finds a compatible library identifier, this tool fetches up-to-date docs/snippets that nested researcher personas and direct users can consume.

## Scope
IN:
- params `libraryId`, `query`
- shared HTTP client usage, request/response normalization, and structured tool output
- clear validation for missing/invalid params and upstream failures
OUT:
